data.title: Laredo Oil reveals tech to access stranded oil reserves, vital for growing energy demand. #oil #LRDC #economy
data.description: In Austin, Texas on July 31, 2025, Laredo Oil, Inc. highlights the ongoing significance of oil as a crucial energy source for both the domestic and global economy. With expectations of increasing demand over the next 20 years, Laredo Oil (OTC: LRDC) aims to leverage this trend for its benefit.

data.title: Vancity saw strong Q2 results, with core revenues reaching $307.5M (+24% from 2024), $46.6M income before tax, $97.8M in member deposits, and $387.2M net retail mortgage lending. Vancity 2.0 vision is on track to enhance member experience and community impact.
data.description: Vancity, based in Vancouver, British Columbia, presented strong financial results for the second quarter ending on June 30, 2025. Core revenues reached $307.5 million, marking a 24% increase from 2024, with $157.6 million added in the second quarter. Income before tax and distributions rose to $46.6 million year-to-date, with $25.1 million added in the second quarter. Member deposits increased by $97.8 million, $63.5 million of which was added in the second quarter. Net retail mortgage lending increased by $387.2 million since the beginning of the year. Approximately 30% of Vancity's net profits are reinvested back into members and communities. President and CEO Wellington Holbrook expressed satisfaction with the results, attributing them to their new growth strategy and commitment to serving members and communities. This success allows Vancity to provide more resources to support people during uncertain times.

data.title: Huey Magoo's expands nationwide with first airport location in Miami, introduces new compact store design for franchisees.
data.description: A prominent player in the fast casual industry has achieved a significant growth milestone in Miami-Dade County by opening several new units in the first and second quarters. The company has introduced a new compact store design with the goal of reducing costs for potential franchisees and expediting the buildout process. The development was announced in Orlando on July 31, 2025.

data.title: F.N.B. Corp (NYSE: FNB) declares $0.12 quarterly cash dividend on common stock, payable Sept 15, 2025.
data.description: Pittsburgh-based F.N.B. Corporation (NYSE: FNB) has disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share for its common stock. The dividend will be disbursed on September 15, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of the market close on a specified date in July.

data.title: Iran weak after Hamas attack on Israel (Oct. 7, 2023) – stocks hit. Post: Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Following Hamas's attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023, Iran's strategic status has significantly diminished. Market stocks were affected as a result of Israel being targeted. This incident has positioned Iran as increasingly vulnerable, with its strength called into question. The attack has garnered global attention, and the impact on Iran's regional influence is notable.

data.title: US-China trade deal raises tariffs to 55%, resolves rare earths issue. Details on China's benefits unclear.
data.description: President Trump has announced that the U.S.-China trade deal will increase tariffs on Chinese goods to 55%, with a resolution reached on rare earths. The agreement raises questions about what China will receive in return.

data.title: Household survey shows decrease in employment and labor force participation. S&P 500 rises despite labor market cooling.
data.description: According to recent data from a household survey, there has been a significant decrease in both employment rates and labor force participation. Despite this decline, the latest Jobs Report indicates a reasonable level of hirings, leading to a rise in the S&P 500. This suggests that while hiring may appear positive, the overall labor market is actually experiencing a cooling trend.

data.title: Weakest private hiring since March 2023; Trump criticizes Fed; S&P 500 rises.
data.description: Hiring by private companies showed its lowest growth rate since March 2023. Following a disappointing ADP jobs report, former President Trump criticized the Federal Reserve. Despite this, the S&P 500 saw an increase in value.

data.title: U.S.-China trade talks in jeopardy as tech war escalates; S&P 500 futures fall amid accusations on deal violations.
data.description: The trade truce between the United States and China is at risk as both countries accused each other of breaching the agreement. The tension has escalated particularly in the tech sector. The S&P 500 futures have fallen following these developments. This situation impacts various aspects of the U.S.-China relationship and may have potential economic repercussions. Stay tuned for further updates on this unfolding situation.

data.title: Fed inflation rate hits 4-year low, S&P 500 dips. Inflation at lowest since March 2021.
data.description: The latest Federal Reserve inflation rate has dropped to its lowest point in four years since March 2021. This decrease in the key rate had a direct impact on the S&P 500, causing a dip in its performance. The decline in the inflation rate is noteworthy for investors as it signifies a significant shift in the economic landscape.

data.title: Trump's copper tariffs cause a boom, with copper hitting record high of $5.85/lb, rising 15.5% month-to-date. US COMEX copper futures trading at 30% premium due to supply panic.
data.description: The price of copper reached a new record high of $5.85 per pound on July 23, representing a 15.5% increase for the month. This spike, tracked by the United States Copper Index Fund, is driven not by economic factors but by political events. President Trump's decision to impose a 50% tariff on copper imports on July 8 has caused disruptions in global metal markets, with U.S. COMEX copper futures now trading at a 30% premium compared to London Metal Exchange contracts. This tariff has made foreign copper imports more expensive, leading to supply shortages and a surge in domestic demand. The article suggests that this unprecedented situation has triggered a supply panic in the copper market.

data.title: U.S. may relax chip restrictions in trade talks with China over rare earths exports; Wall Street predicts.
data.description: Recent discussions between the United States and China regarding trade have become pressing due to Beijing's limitation on rare earth exports. Analysts from Wall Street suggest that in order to address this issue, the U.S. may consider easing constraints on chip exports. This potential trade-off has garnered attention as a possible resolution in the ongoing negotiations.

data.title: Ontario Council of Hospital Unions supports CUPE 5525 members at Villa Colombo Homes, rallying at 3050 Dufferin Street in Toronto.
data.description: In Toronto, negotiations between CUPE 5525 and Villa Colombo Homes for the Aged are ongoing, with the picket line at 3050 Dufferin Street to be supported by the Ontario Council of Hospital Unions. Michael Hurley, President of OCHU, expressed concern for the workers, stating they have been left on the picket line for an extended period. CUPE Ontario and all CUPE members stand in solidarity with these workers. The reinforcement by OCHU aims to provide additional support to the ongoing protests.

data.title: Confluent stock dropped 30% after Q2 results as data company missed out on AI buzz.
data.description: Shares of Confluent experienced a significant drop in value on Wednesday evening, even though the data software firm reported second-quarter earnings that surpassed expectations. The stock plummeted by 30%, reflecting investor disappointment. Confluent's absence from the excitement surrounding artificial intelligence (AI) likely contributed to the decline.

data.title: SQUARE ENIX announces two new games: OCTOPATH TRAVELER 0 set for Dec. 4, 2025 & The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales.
data.description: In Los Angeles, SQUARE ENIX announced two upcoming games at its internal studio, Team Asano, during Nintendo Direct. The first one is OCTOPATH TRAVELER 0, an HD-2D turn-based RPG with a unique storyline preceding the original OCTOPATH TRAVELER. This game is set to release on December 4, 2025. The second game revealed is The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales, an action-packed single-player RPG known for its captivating narrative, extensive exploration opportunities, diverse customization features, and thrilling real-time combat experiences. Keep an eye out for these exciting games from SQUARE ENIX.

data.title: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals beats Q2 earnings estimates with $773.69 million in sales, stock hits 52-week high. Analysts expected loss of 79 cents, while company reported adjusted earnings of 32 cents. Stock is surging.
data.description: Investor optimism in the biotechnology sector is on the rise, with Alnylam Pharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ: ALNY) leading the way by surpassing market expectations. The company's strong position in the competitive landscape is evident as ALNY stock hits its 52-week high. Following an impressive second-quarter performance, Alnylam reported adjusted earnings of 32 cents for Q2 2025, compared to 56 cents the previous year, exceeding analysts' expectations of a 79-cent loss. The company's quarterly sales reached $773.69 million, a 17% increase from last year and surpassing the consensus of $638.55 million. Alnylam Pharmaceuticals is experiencing a surge in its stock value post-earnings report, driven by upbeat financial results and promising 2025 guidance. For more details, visit a financial news website.

data.title: NakedMD, Deorro, and MERZ Aesthetics host charity event in Phoenix on August 22 to support children with serious illnesses.
data.description: A charity concert is scheduled for August 22 at Warehouse 215 in Phoenix. The event aims to raise funds for children dealing with severe illnesses. NakedMD revealed a collaboration with DJ Deorro and MERZ Aesthetics for this cause. The concert plans to attract a large audience, with significant proceeds expected to support the children in need.

data.title: Over 40 workers from Open Kitchens in Chicago join Teamsters Local 703 for improved pay and conditions.
data.description: Nearly fifty drivers and assistants at Open Kitchens in Chicago recently made the decision to become part of Teamsters Local 703, showing their commitment to achieving better working conditions. This group came together to push for improved pay, benefits, and job security. This move signifies a step towards collective action, as the employees unite to negotiate their first Teamsters contract. The decision came after a successful vote involving over 40 workers in total. The newly formed collective is now focused on advocating for their rights and ensuring fair treatment within the workplace.
